content.boundary: Bounded to compositional-sorting, anti-segregation mechanisms including legal-accountability obligations, and coordination of adjacent-domain levers; does not itself redesign SEN-specific identification rules or finance formulas covered elsewhere
content.change_lever: Coordinated package combining anti-segregation/school-choice safeguards with cross-domain policy alignment covering school-readiness rules, rural network planning, and private-sector growth monitoring
content.name: Coordinated anti-segregation and adjacent-policy alignment package
content.system_problem: Free parental school choice and selective tracks remain unaddressed by the 2023 anti-segregation law; Roma children are disproportionately classified as SEN and placed in segregated settings; unresolved ECtHR and EU infringement obligations persist; and non-SEN policies (school-readiness rule changes, rural single-class-school structure, private/church school expansion) measurably move identification and capacity without being designed to do so
